# Pitmaster Tools
> Free BBQ calculators for smoking times, yield, serving, brining, seasoning, charcoal, and catering.

Pitmaster Tools provides browser-based calculators for low-and-slow BBQ cooking. All tools are free, require no account, and run entirely client-side.

## Tools

- [Meat Smoking Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/) — smoking times, timelines, wood pairings, serving estimates for brisket, pork butt, ribs, chicken, turkey
- [Brisket Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/brisket-calculator) — cook time, stall prediction, wrap-method adjustment, sliced vs. pulled
- [Pork Shoulder Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/pork-shoulder-calculator) — pulled pork and sliced pork butt cook times, stall, and timeline
- [Rib Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/rib-calculator) — baby back and spare rib times, 3-2-1 method, doneness indicators
- [Turkey Smoking Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/turkey-smoking-calculator) — spatchcock and whole turkey cook times, temp targets
- [Meat Per Person Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/meat-per-person) — raw-to-cooked yield, serving estimates for any crowd size
- [Cook Time Coordinator](https://pitmaster.tools/cook-time-coordinator) — multi-meat simultaneous finish timing
- [Brine Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/brine-calculator) — wet and dry brine ratios by weight
- [Dry Rub Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/dry-rub-calculator) — scale any rub recipe to exact meat weight
- [Charcoal Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/charcoal-calculator) — charcoal and wood chunk estimates by cook length and smoker type
- [BBQ Cost Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/bbq-cost-calculator) — cost per serving and total cook budget
- [Catering Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/catering-calculator) — raw weight, sides, and total cost for catered events
- [Brisket Yield Calculator](https://pitmaster.tools/brisket-yield-calculator) — how much brisket to buy for a target cooked yield
- [Best Smoke Days Forecast](https://pitmaster.tools/smoke-weather/) — 7-day weather forecast scored 0-100 for your cut and cooker, by ZIP
- [Best Cities to Smoke Meat Today](https://pitmaster.tools/smoke-weather/best-cities) — all 50 U.S. metros ranked by today's smoke score, updated daily
- [Smoking Times & Temperatures Chart](https://pitmaster.tools/smoking-times-and-temps) — free printable cook times, smoker and pull temps, rest, and USDA safe minimums

<!-- GUIDES:START -->
## Guides

- [How to Manage the Brisket Stall (Without Panicking)](https://pitmaster.tools/guides/techniques/managing-the-stall) — The stall is where most first briskets fall apart. Here's what it actually is, why it happens, and the three ways to get through it without ruining the cook.
<!-- GUIDES:END -->

## Key Facts

- Brisket cooks at ~1.25–1.5 hrs/lb at 225°F; pull at 195°F (sliced) or 205°F (pulled)
- Pork shoulder: ~1.5 hrs/lb at 225°F; pull at 205°F for pulled pork
- Baby back ribs: 5–5.5 hrs at 225°F; spare ribs: 6–6.5 hrs
- Brisket loses ~40% weight during cooking; pork butt ~35%
- Minimum brisket rest: 90 minutes; ideal rest: 2–4 hours
- Safe minimum internal temperature for poultry: 165°F (USDA)
- Safe minimum for pork: 145°F with 3-minute rest (USDA)
- Each metro forecast page (/smoke-weather/<city>) includes monthly climate normals — average high/low, wind, humidity, and rainy days — with a 0-100 smoke-day score per month. Temperature and rain days are NOAA 1991-2020 U.S. Climate Normals; wind and humidity are 2015-2024 reanalysis averages. Per-metro data is downloadable at /smoke-weather/<city>-normals.json
